RMK_TEXT: AIRCRAFT WAS PARKED DOING A RUN-UP AND WAS STRUCK BY N43000, GALVESTON, TX.
LANDED ON RUNWAY 24 BUT WAS UNABLE TO STOP, VEERED OFF THE LEFT SIDE OF THE RUNWAY, AND OVERRAN A PORTION OF THE EMAS. AIRCRAFT REPORTED BRAKE ISSUE
MADE A GEAR UP LANDING ON RUNWAY 27 FOR UNKNOWN REASONS.

DECLARED AN EMERGENCY DUE TO A BIRD STRIKE AND RETURNED TO SAT.  AIRCRAFT LANDED AND TAXIED TO PARKING WITHOUT FURTHER
INCIDENT. PILOT REPORTED DAMAGE TO THE LEFT WINGLET.

(INSTRUCTOR/STUDENT) HAD A HARD LANDING DURING TOUCH-N-GO, DETACHING RIGHT MAIN GEAR (WHICH WAS RELAYED TO PILOT BY SOMEONE ON THE GROUND VIA CTAF). ACFT LANDED JUST SHORT OF RWY36, DOING FURTHER DAMAGE TO ACFT
DECLARED AN EMERGENCY DUE TO A LEFT RUDDER ISSUE AND DIVERTED TO MKE. ACFT LANDED AND VEERED OFF RWY 31,RESULTING IN A NOSE GEAR COLLAPSE AND PROP STRIKE
REPORTED ENGINE ISSUES DURING TAKEOFF AND CLIPPED TREES AT THE END OF THE RUNWAY. AIRCRAFT CAME TO REST IN THE TREES.

DURING DEPARTURE RWY10, ACFT VEERED LEFT (NEVER ROTATED) INTO THE GRASS, AND HIT A POTHOLE OR DITCH, RESULTING IN
DETACHMENT OF THE NOSE WHEEL. PILOT APPLIED RIGHT RUDDER, BUT WITH NO EFFECT.
DURING LANDING RWY 17, ACFT VEERED OFF THE LEFT SIDE OF THE RUNWAY AND FLIPPED INVERTED.
